jokes.text: We don't talk, and it's good that way.
jokes.setup: Mother offers to talk during laundry
jokes.punchline: Charlie's blunt rejection of bonding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie character moment - antisocial and direct. Great economy with genuine character truth.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We don't talk, and it's good that way.

jokes.text: Ooh, talky, talky, talky, talky, talky.
jokes.setup: Charlie's mom wants to discuss something serious
jokes.punchline: Mac's dismissive baby talk
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's immaturity and dismissiveness, but fairly basic humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Ooh, talky, talky, talky, talky, talky.

jokes.text: Um, I have cancer. / Yeah, but I got to... I'm sorry. What?
jokes.setup: Casual cancer revelation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's distracted non-reaction then delayed processing
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy timing - cancer announced casually, met with distraction. Structure is flawless.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: Um, I have cancer.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I got to... I'm sorry. What?

jokes.text: But how? You don't even smoke.
jokes.setup: Mother has lung cancer
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confused logic about cancer causation
jokes.explanation: Charlie's naive understanding of cancer is character-appropriate but not groundbreaking.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: But how? You don't even smoke.

jokes.text: How many pairs of shoes have you lost this month, Frank? / Three. / Hmm? / Four.
jokes.setup: Frank calling someone else mentally unstable
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately contradicts himself about lost shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment showing his declining mental state through simple contradiction.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: How many pairs of shoes have you lost this month, Frank?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Three.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hmm?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Four.

jokes.text: The point is my mind is as sharp as a... what do you call it? Um, it's a... Knife? No, uh, what you put... What you... What you...
jokes.setup: Frank claiming his mind is sharp
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgetting the word 'tack' mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Beautiful ironic structure - claiming sharpness while demonstrating the opposite. Classic Sunny contradiction.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The point is my mind is as sharp as a... what do you call it? Um, it's a... Knife? No, uh, what you put... What you... What you...

jokes.text: That didn't sound very genuine. / No. Sorry. / What was I supposed to do?
jokes.setup: Mac's hollow sympathy for cancer news
jokes.punchline: Mac admitting his insincerity when called out
jokes.explanation: Mac's casual sociopathy shown through his inability to fake empathy properly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That didn't sound very genuine.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No. Sorry. What was I supposed to do?

jokes.text: I think the cancer thing, for whatever reason, is just not... You know, it's just not grabbing me right now.
jokes.setup: Friend's mother has cancer
jokes.punchline: Mac treats it like entertainment that's not engaging enough
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Mac moment - treating cancer like bad TV. Dark but character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I think the cancer thing, for whatever reason, is just not... You know, it's just not grabbing me right now.

jokes.text: My mom is dying! / Okay, calm down, calm down.
jokes.setup: Charlie's emotional outburst about his dying mother
jokes.punchline: Mac's dismissive 'calm down' response
jokes.explanation: Mac's complete lack of empathy shown economically. Typical awful friend behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: My mom is dying!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, calm down, calm down.

jokes.text: My mom has a doctor that can cure the cancer for 4,200 bucks, so no big deal.
jokes.setup: Cancer is serious and expensive to treat
jokes.punchline: Suspiciously specific low price for cancer cure
jokes.explanation: The specific dollar amount and 'no big deal' makes this obviously a scam, showing Charlie's naivety.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: My mom has a doctor that can cure the cancer for 4,200 bucks, so no big deal.

jokes.text: Thumbtack. / What? / Thumbtack. / We were talking about things that earlier that were sharp, and a thumbtack is sharp.
jokes.setup: Frank forgot the word earlier, conversation has moved on completely
jokes.punchline: Frank randomly remembers 'thumbtack' minutes later
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Frank's brain lag. The timing makes it absurd and character-revealing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Thumbtack.
What?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Thumbtack. We were talking about things that earlier that were sharp, and a thumbtack is sharp.

jokes.text: Dr. Jinx is the name of a monkey, not a man.
jokes.setup: Suspicious doctor's name revealed
jokes.punchline: Mac pointing out it sounds like a cartoon animal
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor that highlights how obviously fake this doctor is.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dr. Jinx is the name of a monkey, not a man.

jokes.text: He lives in a garage?
jokes.setup: Looking for professional medical help
jokes.punchline: Realizing the 'doctor' operates from a garage
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction to obvious red flag. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He lives in a garage?

jokes.text: When most people think of a doctor, they don't think of a black man living in a garage surrounded by houseplants. / I normally think Asian. / Yeah, or I think of an Indian guy. / Or white. / Middle Eastern. Pretty much anything other than black.
jokes.setup: Dr. Jinx acknowledges racial assumptions about doctors
jokes.punchline: Gang casually lists every other race as more doctor-like
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about racism that shows the gang's casual prejudice. Risky but character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Jinx
dialogue.line: When most people think of a doctor, they don't think of a black man living in a garage surrounded by houseplants.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I normally think Asian.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, or I think of an Indian guy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Or white.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Middle Eastern. Pretty much anything other than black.

jokes.text: Well, that's not eczema. What you have there, my friend, that's sailor's rot.
jokes.setup: Mac seeking treatment for skin condition
jokes.punchline: Fake doctor's made-up nautical disease diagnosis
jokes.explanation: 'Sailor's rot' is a brilliantly absurd fake medical term that sounds just plausible enough.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Jinx
dialogue.line: Well, that's not eczema. What you have there, my friend, that's sailor's rot.

jokes.text: When was the last time you've been to Haiti? / Never. I've never been to... I've been to Kensington.
jokes.setup: Exotic tropical disease inquiry
jokes.punchline: Mac's only travel is to rough Philly neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Good local humor - Kensington as Mac's idea of exotic travel shows his limited worldview.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Jinx
dialogue.line: When was the last time you've been to Haiti?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Never. I've never been to... I've been to Kensington.

jokes.text: Do you have anything from the Mother Earth that would be sprayed on me and cause me to have feelings again 'cause I'm having trouble feeling things.
jokes.setup: Seeking medical help from fake doctor
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually admits to emotional numbness as a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis sociopathy - treating lack of empathy like a medical condition to cure with herbs.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Do you have anything from the Mother Earth that would be sprayed on me and cause me to have feelings again 'cause I'm having trouble feeling things.

jokes.text: I think he sprayed me with Miracle-Gro.
jokes.setup: Fake doctor's 'Mother Earth' treatment
jokes.punchline: Realizing it's just plant fertilizer
jokes.explanation: Simple reveal of the scam's ridiculousness. Economical and clear.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I think he sprayed me with Miracle-Gro.

jokes.text: Oh, she keeps a cigarette burning out on the steps outside, so she's gonna go hack a butt.
jokes.setup: Charlie's mom supposedly has lung cancer
jokes.punchline: She's constantly smoking cigarettes during church
jokes.explanation: Ironic juxtaposition of lung cancer patient chain-smoking. Character detail that hints at the scam.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, she keeps a cigarette burning out on the steps outside, so she's gonna go hack a butt.

jokes.text: This is a scam, okay? This is a scam. It's kneeling, standing, getting people all confused.
jokes.setup: Catholic mass with its traditional rituals
jokes.punchline: Mac seeing liturgical movements as a con game
jokes.explanation: Mac's reductive view of Catholic ritual as intentional confusion shows his paranoid mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This is a scam, okay? This is a scam. It's kneeling, standing, getting people all confused.

jokes.text: I'm getting a Fa... No, I'm getting a Ba... No, I'm getting a Da... Dennis? / Dennis! / Is it? / Yes, yeah, no, I'm seeing that.
jokes.setup: Psychic claiming supernatural knowledge
jokes.punchline: Obviously fishing for information with alphabet guessing
jokes.explanation: Standard cold reading satire. Shows how obvious the scam is but Dee falls for it anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Psychic John
dialogue.line: I'm getting a Fa... No, I'm getting a Ba... No, I'm getting a Da... Dennis?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dennis!

dialogue.speaker: Psychic John
dialogue.line: Is it?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yes, yeah, no, I'm seeing that.

jokes.text: Ha-- I was just out buying new shoes and I remembered I didn't want to give you that hundred bucks. / Yeah, and then you called me and you said you changed your mind and you'd like to give me another hundred, so where is it? / I did? / Yeah. / Ah, shit.
jokes.setup: Frank remembers not wanting to pay the psychic
jokes.punchline: Psychic convinces confused Frank he already agreed to pay more
jokes.explanation: Shows how Frank's declining memory makes him easy to scam. Good character exploitation.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Ha-- I was just out buying new shoes and I remembered I didn't want to give you that hundred bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Psychic John
dialogue.line: Yeah, and then you called me and you said you changed your mind and you'd like to give me another hundred, so where is it?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I did?

dialogue.speaker: Psychic John
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Ah, shit.

jokes.text: You have a pork chop bone in your left pocket; you have 57 cents in your right, and you clipped someone's side mirror while parking in a handicap spot.
jokes.setup: Psychic trying to prove his abilities
jokes.punchline: Specific details about Frank's awful behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank details - pork chop bone is gross/specific, handicap parking shows his awfulness.

dialogue.speaker: Psychic John
dialogue.line: You have a pork chop bone in your left pocket; you have 57 cents in your right, and you clipped someone's side mirror while parking in a handicap spot.

jokes.text: Can you conjure the dead? Can you get my dead whore wife back here so I can lay into her from the other side?
jokes.setup: Frank asking about psychic's powers
jokes.punchline: Wants to yell at dead wife from beyond grave
jokes.explanation: Peak Frank awfulness - wanting to continue arguments after death. Dark, specific, and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Can you conjure the dead? Can you get my dead whore wife back here so I can lay into her from the other side?

jokes.text: I am feeling as if she is still living somewhere in the city with a small Mexican dog.
jokes.setup: Psychic claiming to sense dead wife's spirit
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she faked death and has oddly specific pet
jokes.explanation: 'Small Mexican dog' is a wonderfully specific absurd detail that makes the lie more believable.

dialogue.speaker: Psychic John
dialogue.line: I am feeling as if she is still living somewhere in the city with a small Mexican dog.

jokes.text: You're telling me that you believe that Christ comes back to life every Sunday in the form of a bowl of crackers, and then you proceed to just eat the man?
jokes.setup: Catholic communion explained
jokes.punchline: Dennis describing transubstantiation as cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Classic atheist take on communion, but Dennis's clinical delivery makes it darker.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're telling me that you believe that Christ comes back to life every Sunday in the form of a bowl of crackers, and then you proceed to just eat the man?

jokes.text: Hey, guys, look at this. My rash is almost completely cleared up. / Oh, my God. / Whoa. / Yeah, look at that. Whatever he sprayed on me worked.
jokes.setup: Dr. Jinx was obviously a fake
jokes.punchline: His treatment accidentally worked
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reversal - the fake doctor's treatment worked by accident, complicating their certainty.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, guys, look at this. My rash is almost completely cleared up.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, my God.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Whoa.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, look at that. Whatever he sprayed on me worked.

jokes.text: Charlie, my mother is dying of cancer and you need money to fix a statue? / No, no, no, no, don't give me this act. / Charlie, calm down. / No, no, no, why don't you give us some money, okay?
jokes.setup: Church asking for donations during crisis
jokes.punchline: Charlie aggressively demanding money from priest instead
jokes.explanation: Charlie's emotional breakdown leading to inappropriate aggression toward clergy is peak cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: Charlie, my mother is dying of cancer and you need money to fix a statue?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no, don't give me this act.

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: Charlie, calm down.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, no, no, why don't you give us some money, okay?

jokes.text: It's the Holy Trinity: beef and beer and Jesus.
jokes.setup: Explaining their fundraising scheme
jokes.punchline: Replacing Father/Son/Holy Spirit with party food
jokes.explanation: Clever blasphemous wordplay with perfect economy. Quintessential Mac mixing religion with schemes.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's the Holy Trinity: beef and beer and Jesus.

jokes.text: Aw, there's a dead dog in there! / Gross! / That's not a dog. That's my toupee, my blond toupee. I used to wear this in Miami.
jokes.setup: Digging up what they think is a buried dog
jokes.punchline: It's Frank's old hairpiece, mistaken for a corpse
jokes.explanation: Perfect gross-out misdirection. The Miami detail adds character history and makes it more absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Aw, there's a dead dog in there!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Gross!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's not a dog. That's my toupee, my blond toupee. I used to wear this in Miami.

jokes.text: Padre! So good to see you. So good of you to come. / Oh... Yeah, right on, right on. / Well, it's for a good cause, isn't it? So... have some beef, have some beer. And if you feel the calling, you know, go ahead and pop that ring in the donation bucket. Although I know you won't.
jokes.setup: Welcoming priest to their fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Immediately challenging him to prove his charity while assuming he won't
jokes.explanation: Mac's passive-aggressive approach to the priest shows his resentment about church donation requests.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Padre! So good to see you. So good of you to come.

dialogue.speaker: Priest
dialogue.line: Oh... Yeah, right on, right on.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, it's for a good cause, isn't it? So... have some beef, have some beer. And if you feel the calling, you know, go ahead and pop that ring in the donation bucket. Although I know you won't.

jokes.text: Mom, you want people to feel sorry for you, right? Then they give a lot of money. And you just don't look that sick. You gotta look sicker.
jokes.setup: Helping mother prepare for fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Charlie critiquing his cancer-patient mother's appearance for not being pathetic enough
jokes.explanation: Peak awful son behavior - treating his mother's cancer as a marketing problem. Perfectly horrible.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Mom, you want people to feel sorry for you, right? Then they give a lot of money. And you just don't look that sick. You gotta look sicker.

jokes.text: But I feel like it's lying. / Oh, it's not lying, Mrs. Kelly, because you are dying of cancer. / Yeah. / It's devouring your insides.
jokes.setup: Mother feeling guilty about fake makeup
jokes.punchline: Dennis and Charlie reassuring her by describing cancer graphically
jokes.explanation: Dennis's clinical description of cancer to comfort her shows his warped empathy attempts.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: But I feel like it's lying.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, it's not lying, Mrs. Kelly, because you are dying of cancer.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's devouring your insides.

jokes.text: It's about whether or not we're dumb enough to believe in it that matters.
jokes.setup: Dennis giving speech about faith
jokes.punchline: Reducing belief to stupidity while addressing believers
jokes.explanation: Dennis accidentally insulting his audience while trying to manipulate them. Perfect sociopathic slip.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's about whether or not we're dumb enough to believe in it that matters.

jokes.text: Do you feel the invisible things around you that don't really exist? Oh, it doesn't matter!
jokes.setup: Dennis building religious fervor
jokes.punchline: Explicitly calling spiritual feelings fake while encouraging them
jokes.explanation: Dennis's manipulation becomes so cynical he's openly mocking religion while preaching it.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Do you feel the invisible things around you that don't really exist? Oh, it doesn't matter!

jokes.text: Dennis, that was amazing, dude. I am so proud of you. You are feeling again. No. I meant none of it.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinking Dennis had a genuine spiritual moment
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately clarifying it was all manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure - builds hope then crushes it with Dennis's casual sociopathy. Excellent character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dennis, that was amazing, dude. I am so proud of you. You are feeling again.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No. I meant none of it.

jokes.text: As you know, I'm as sick as a three-legged dog on the streets of India.
jokes.setup: Mother reading prepared cancer speech
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and culturally inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific comparison that's both vivid and inappropriately exotic. Classic Sunny over-writing.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: As you know, I'm as sick as a three-legged dog on the streets of India.

jokes.text: Any minute, my lungs are going to sizzle, pop, and disintegrate into a liquid lung and organ gumbo soup.
jokes.setup: Describing cancer progression
jokes.punchline: Turning disease into grotesque food metaphor
jokes.explanation: 'Gumbo soup' is wonderfully disgusting and absurd. The gang's over-written speech backfires spectacularly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: Any minute, my lungs are going to sizzle, pop, and disintegrate into a liquid lung and organ gumbo soup.

jokes.text: Give me money. Money me. Money now. Me a money needing a lot now.
jokes.setup: Mother supposed to make eloquent plea for donations
jokes.punchline: Charlie's caveman-level writing exposed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his broken English reveals his simple mind. Became iconic and quotable. Peak Sunny writing.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: Give me money. Money me. Money now. Me a money needing a lot now.

jokes.text: We're the ones that broke the Virgin Mary statue.
jokes.setup: Supposed to be asking for cancer treatment money
jokes.punchline: Reveals the real reason they need money
jokes.explanation: Great plot revelation that recontextualizes everything. The scam collapses through guilty conscience.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: We're the ones that broke the Virgin Mary statue.

jokes.text: I never actually said I was a doctor. / He is the goddamn church gardener.
jokes.setup: Confronting the fake doctor
jokes.punchline: He never claimed to be a doctor - they assumed it
jokes.explanation: Smart reversal - the 'victim' reveals they scammed themselves through assumptions. Good con artist logic.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Jinx
dialogue.line: I never actually said I was a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He is the goddamn church gardener.

jokes.text: You had my back up against the wall, I had to spray you with a heavy dose of pesticide.
jokes.setup: Mac's rash mysteriously healing
jokes.punchline: Fake doctor admits to using poison that accidentally worked
jokes.explanation: Completes the pesticide callback while adding more absurdity to the fake doctor's methods.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Jinx
dialogue.line: You had my back up against the wall, I had to spray you with a heavy dose of pesticide.

jokes.text: Dennis. Dennis! / Mom's alive. She faked her own death to steal Frank's money, and we think it's buried in her grave.
jokes.setup: Frank's erratic behavior throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Reveals elaborate conspiracy theory involving fake death
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoid theory perfectly fits his declining mental state and the show's conspiracy logic.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis. Dennis!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Mom's alive. She faked her own death to steal Frank's money, and we think it's buried in her grave.

jokes.text: Ah, shit! No! No! Mommy, Mommy! / Ah! I grifted ya! / What are you talking about? / I paid off the psychic. I buried the money in the dog grave. My mommy, my mommy's a skeleton!
jokes.setup: Opening what they think is Frank's wife's grave
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals elaborate prank while Charlie finds actual corpse
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy climax - Frank's proud grift reveal coincides with Charlie's genuine horror. Masterful structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Ah, shit! No! No! Mommy, Mommy!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Ah! I grifted ya!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I paid off the psychic. I buried the money in the dog grave. My mommy, my mommy's a skeleton!

jokes.text: You made me dig up my dead mother because I said you were losing it? / Score one for the old people. Ha ha!
jokes.setup: Charlie realizes Frank's 'proof' involved grave desecration
jokes.punchline: Frank treating it as a victory for senior citizens
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete lack of remorse for making Charlie desecrate his mother's grave is peak sociopathy. Perfectly awful.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You made me dig up my dead mother because I said you were losing it?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Score one for the old people. Ha ha!
